🎯 Management Attention

generated 2026-07-20 12:39 UTC · guidelines: docs/incident-handling-guidelines.md
Under control?No — 4 items need immediate attention (2 escalate-now, 2 needs-management-attention)
Urgent vs smaller4 urgent, 8 need a nudge, 6 routine
Total open (active, excl. triage)18 -1 vs last digest (19 → 18)
2
🚨 Escalate now
2
🔴 Needs attention
8
🟡 Watch
6
🟢 Under control

🆕 New since last digest

  • none

✅ Resolved since last digest

  • INC-14839 — closed clean — MrBit partner was looking up the wrong FT user ID; deposit confirmed present, resolved in ~47 min, no breaches
Guideline codes legend (docs/incident-handling-guidelines.md)
  • A1 — Every incident has an owner and Head-of-SRE-level visibility
  • A2 — No incident is “just closed” — every problem gets an action
  • A3 — “It's just 10/30 minutes” is never an acceptable rationale
  • A4 — Recurring problems are escalated to the owning team for a long-term fix
  • B5 — Escalate early — not after hours without recovery
  • B6 — Security incidents are P1 until de-escalated
  • B7 — Critical-path failures are top priority
  • B8 — Night and handover incidents must not be lost — assign, don't just mention
  • C9 — Inform partners fast, even before root cause is known
  • C10 — Close the loop — recovery comms sent, partner questions answered
  • C11 — Comms accurate and crystal clear on impact
  • D12 — Informed decisions over passive waiting
  • D13 — Runbooks followed, basic checks done
  • D14 — Don't close while partner-side work remains open
  • D15 — The incident record is self-contained and phone-readable
  • E16 — Digest answers: under control? urgent vs smaller? new bugs? how many open?

🚨 Escalate now 2

INC-8064 slack intercomP1open 54d· evidence to 20 Jul 08:31 UTC
[P1] Intercom Incident : H2Bet Production
📝 Management note (2026-07-16) Andrea: correction — H2Bet ACCEPTED the interim workaround (discussed in the meeting with them and our Partner Manager, early Jul); that acceptance is why REW-176 was opened on 9 Jul. The 28 May rejection was the earlier 503-as-success proposal only. No sign-off is pending from H2Bet.

All three breaches behind the 🚨 flag (A2/A4, B5, C10/D15) still hold with zero change this cycle. This reflects the 16 Jul management note (H2Bet accepted the workaround) but the note does not resolve the underlying breaches. Now ~55 days open.

H2Bet duplicate bonus grants confirmed as root cause within hours of the 26 May report, but ~55 days later there is still no deployed fix — roughly 300 duplicate grants/week continue. Per the management note, H2Bet has accepted the interim workaround and REW-176 remains parked on Rewards-team capacity until mid-August. Zero new activity anywhere again this cycle.

Open breaches
  • A2/A4: still holds — ~55 days in, no decisive fix deployed, REW-176 still parked
  • B5: still holds — zero evidence of any BetConstruct response or outreach progress since 18 Jun
  • C10/D15: still holds — Intercom conversation remains closed while incident.io stays open P1 with no incident.io-side explanation of the closure or REW-176 status
Proposed / pending
  • REW-176 interim workaround - ACCEPTED by H2Bet (per management note), still blocked by Rewards capacity until mid-Aug — no movement observed
Timeline (1)
  • 20 Jul run (14:39 cycle): zero new Slack messages, no new Intercom parts, no incident.io update text since the last read — fully static again

→ Force a decision point: get Rewards-team capacity committed to REW-176 with a hard date, or explicitly accept the ~300/week duplicate-grant cost until mid-August and say so in the record.

INC-14476 slack intercomP1open 3d· evidence to 20 Jul 07:04 UTC
[P1] Intercom Incident : Mystake Production

Still 🚨: True P1, partner silent ~74h (3.1 days) since the 17 Jul technical ask despite FT's strongest mitigation to date (recon service fully stopped) and repeated outreach — but the account-manager escalation suggested across 6 prior digests still has not happened, now a 7th cycle. B5's 'system clearly not recovering with no escalation in sight' still applies.

Cocobet (Mystake) blocked users appear to 'unblock' themselves because the partner's daily recon feed omits users who should stay blocked. Joachim sent the partner a clear technical ask on 17 Jul; the partner has been silent since. FT has applied progressively stronger mitigation (origin resync + blacklist, then stopping the recon service entirely) and made two fresh outreach attempts last cycle (Intercom + a direct DM from Ilke) — still no reply. Account-manager escalation still has not happened, now a 6th consecutive digest cycle flagging it.

Open breaches
  • B5: still no account-manager escalation despite 6 consecutive digests suggesting it — system not recovering, only stronger internal mitigation applied
  • C9/C10: partner silent ~74h (3.1 days) since the 17 Jul 10:17 UTC technical ask, despite three FT follow-up attempts (Intercom ×2 + direct DM)
  • D15: incident.io record still doesn't reflect the recon-service-stopped mitigation or the direct DM outreach
Proposed / pending
  • Resync affected origin via user migration and blacklist Cocobet's origin from the daily recon job — applied ~19:47 CEST 17 Jul
  • Stop Cocobet's recon service entirely — applied 06:52 CEST 20 Jul
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-17T08:42
  • root-cause explanation and recon-feed check request — delivered 12:18-12:19 CEST 17 Jul; no partner reply since, confirmed still silent through this run
Timeline (8)
  • 17 Jul 10:17 UTC Joachim sends partner a technical ask — no reply since
  • 17 Jul 19:09-19:47 CEST Giancarlo applies internal mitigation (origin resync + recon blacklist, ~20k players)
  • 18 Jul 20:45 CEST Andrea reassigns Incident Lead Joachim → Giancarlo — no fresh partner outreach accompanied this handoff
  • 20 Jul 06:52 CEST Giancarlo stops the Cocobet recon service entirely to prevent further incorrect unblocks
  • 20 Jul 06:28 UTC Giancarlo follows up again with partner (Sandro) on Intercom — no reply
  • 20 Jul 08:54-09:04 CEST Ilke reaches out to Sandro directly via DM — no reply
  • 20 Jul run (12:30 cycle): zero new Slack or Intercom activity; partner silence since the 17 Jul 10:17 UTC technical ask now ~72h (3.0 days); account-manager escalation still not initiated — 6th consecutive digest cycle flagging it
  • 20 Jul run (14:39 cycle): zero new Slack or Intercom activity; partner silence since the 17 Jul 10:17 UTC technical ask now ~74h (3.1 days); account-manager escalation still not initiated — 7th consecutive digest cycle flagging it

→ Escalate to Cocobet's account manager today — 7th consecutive digest flagging this; direct engineer-to-partner DMs and Intercom follow-ups are not a substitute for a formal account-management escalation on a 74h-silent True P1.

🔴 Needs attention 2

INC-12022 slack intercomP1open 17d· evidence to 20 Jul 08:32 UTC
Shortlinks Redirecting to "Forbidden" page
📝 Management note (2026-07-16) Andrea: Solved as an incident. Only pending item is with Estelarbet, who need to identify which players were impacted — this is being checked and shared from our side; we need their input on which players complained so we can cross-check. Confirmed via the partner Intercom ticket.

Per Andrea's management note this is otherwise solved as an incident, but the partner's 17 Jul 22:35 UTC scope-clarification message has now sat unanswered ~62h (2.6 days) with no re-engagement from FT — a clear, overdue partner-comms breach (C9/C10) the note does not cover, still worsening.

Cloudflare-origin check blocked shortlink/unsubscribe traffic; mitigated same day, fix merged 02 Jul. Per Andrea's management note this is solved as an incident, pending only Estelarbet's cross-check. The partner (Gloria Morocho) clarified on Intercom at 22:35 UTC 17 Jul that the reported error affected ALL urls (not just unsubscribe), across both notifications and emails — reframing the cross-check's scope. FT still has not replied, now ~60 hours (2.5 days) later.

Open breaches
  • C9/C10: partner's 22:35 UTC 17 Jul scope clarification (that the reported error affected ALL urls, not just unsubscribe) remains unanswered ~62h later — well past what should have been a same-day reply
Proposed / pending
  • CF-origin carve-out - merged 02 Jul
  • deliver full partner report - cross-check inconclusive, now needs to incorporate the partner's broader-scope clarification
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-02T13:29
  • last partner message unanswered
  • RCA/report promised repeatedly (13-15 Jul), still not fully delivered
Timeline (3)
  • 17 Jul 22:35 UTC partner (Gloria Morocho) clarifies scope: all urls affected, not only unsubscribe — no FT reply yet
  • 20 Jul run: zero new Intercom/Slack activity; partner's 17 Jul 22:35 UTC scope-clarification message now ~60h (2.5 days) unanswered by FT
  • 20 Jul run (14:39 cycle): zero new Intercom/Slack activity; partner's 17 Jul 22:35 UTC scope-clarification message now ~62h (2.6 days) unanswered by FT

→ Reply to Estelarbet's/Gloria Morocho's 17 Jul scope-clarification message on Intercom today — now over 2.6 days unanswered on an otherwise-solved P1.

INC-11079 slackP2open 23d· evidence to 19 Jul 02:30 UTC
segurobet: no consumers on queue segurobet.consentsmanager (messages: 1, in: 0/s)
📝 Management note (2026-07-16) Andrea: this is in reality a BetConstruct platform issue (their Operator API returns 500 on consent updates). The partner is aware and has raised it with BetConstruct themselves; active partner correspondence (examples provided, regular chasing incl. by Andrea) lives in the partner channel thread: https://fasttrack-solutions.slack.com/archives/C08VB1RBJLF/p1782549375502499 — future runs should treat that thread as the live track, not the quiet incident channel.

Management note (16 Jul) held this at watch-level as a BetConstruct-side blocker with active chasing, with escalation if unanswered much longer — that condition remains met: Andrea's 16 Jul re-chase has had zero reply for ~4.1 days on a P2 open ~23 days with no FT-side mitigation. Zero new activity anywhere this cycle, confirmed via direct thread read.

Segurobet Consent Manager remains stalled since 27 Jun on partner (BetConstruct) Operator API 500s. Per management note, the live track is the partner thread, not this incident channel. No reply there since Andrea's 16 Jul re-chase to Eduardo Nunes (BetConstruct) — now ~96h (4.0 days) unanswered, confirmed via a direct read of the partner thread this cycle.

Open breaches
  • C9/C10: BetConstruct has not responded to Andrea's 16 Jul 12:54 CEST chase in the #ftcrm-segurobet partner thread — now ~98h (4.1 days) of partner silence on an unresolved P2 open since 27 Jun (~23 days); no FT-side mitigation applied in the meantime
Proposed / pending
  • notify partner + supply failing examples - done
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-06-27T08:36
  • last partner message unanswered
  • 'we'll check' (01 Jul) - no ETA ever given; Andrea's 16 Jul re-chase remains unanswered
Timeline (4)
  • 16 Jul 12:54 CEST: Andrea's re-chase to Eduardo Nunes (BetConstruct) — still unanswered
  • 19 Jul 04:30 CEST: James Hodgson left the incident channel
  • 20 Jul run: zero new activity in incident channel; direct read of the #ftcrm-segurobet partner thread confirms no reply since Andrea's 16 Jul 12:54 CEST re-chase — now ~96h (4.0 days) of partner silence
  • 20 Jul run (14:39 cycle): zero new activity in incident channel; direct re-read of the #ftcrm-segurobet partner thread confirms still no reply since Andrea's 16 Jul 12:54 CEST re-chase — now ~98h (4.1 days) of partner silence

→ Escalate directly with BetConstruct account management — now ~4.1 days of silence on Andrea's own re-chase, on a 23-day-old P2 with player consent updates still not applying in real time.

🟡 Watch 8

INC-10045 slack intercomP2open 31d· evidence to 17 Jul 09:55 UTC
[P2] Intercom Incident : Power Play Production

Continued thorough, collaborative investigation with Integration/PM engaged, but the internal decision (scoping answer / what to tell the partner) has now been pending since Friday (3 days) despite Matteo's fresh push today; the Intercom SLA remains formally missed — worth a direct check next cycle if it still hasn't landed.

GiG (Power Play's upstream KYC) event-ordering bug leaves stale block tags. The scoping answer Robert asked Stephen/Curt for on 17 Jul is still outstanding; Matteo has now directly nudged Stephen and Robert for a decision so the partner can be told what to expect and the ticket handed to Lis — the internal decision point has been open since Friday (3 days) with no resolution yet.

Proposed / pending
  • update_epoch as ordering key — Robert asked Stephen/Curt to scope size of this fallback vs pushing GiG to fix event ordering themselves; scoping answer still pending
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-06-16T12:16
  • notify once re-syncs complete - kept
  • structural handover to Integration/PM drafted 16 Jul, sent to Robert/Lis/Gui 17 Jul
Timeline (2)

  • 20 Jul 13:47 CEST Matteo nudges Stephen Riolo and Robert Friberg for a decision on what was discussed Friday, wants to finalize what to tell the partner, and hand over to Lis — no reply yet within this run window

→ Stephen/Robert to give Matteo a same-day answer so partner comms and the handover to Lis can proceed — decision has been pending since Friday.

INC-14346 slackP2open 4d· evidence to 17 Jul 21:42 UTC
CF Threat Watch: brute-force-suspect,login-success-after-fails on ft-crm.com (41.76.35.208)

Handling was fast and correct (B6 escalation, false-positive determination), but the record has now sat unclosed ~4.0 days since the conclusion settled — still a minor D15 record-hygiene gap, not a live incident concern.

CF Threat Watch brute-force-suspect alert on ft-crm.com. Correct B6 security escalation flow despite paging misfires; team read is a shared-network false positive per Cloudflare's own classification. Status remains 'Investigating' with no incident.io updates since 2026-07-16T12:40:33Z; incident.io's own auto-nudge on 17 Jul asking to mark it resolved remains unactioned, now ~4.3 days since the false-positive conclusion settled.

Open breaches
  • D15: incident sitting in 'Investigating' ~4.3 days with the conclusion settled (shared-network false positive) and the automated resolve-nudge still unactioned
Timeline (5)
  • 16 Jul 12:10 — alert fires, incident opened, escalated to P2 Investigating within minutes
  • 16 Jul 12:40 — last incident.io status update; team settles on shared-network false-positive read
  • 17 Jul 23:42 CEST — incident.io bot auto-prompts to mark resolved — still no reply
  • 20 Jul run: zero new Slack activity; resolve-nudge from 17 Jul still unactioned, now ~4.3 days since the false-positive conclusion settled
  • 20 Jul run (14:39 cycle): zero new Slack activity; resolve-nudge from 17 Jul still unactioned, now ~4.0 days since the false-positive conclusion settled

→ Stephen Riolo to click resolve/close now that the false-positive conclusion is settled.

INC-14640 slackP2open 44h· evidence to 20 Jul 06:43 UTC
[FIRING:1] Rewards: GraphQL API errors (Grafana) Alerting (true rewards wemalaysia: TIMEOUT)

🟡 — the Monday follow-up with Ryan Yu, agreed on 18 Jul, still hasn't been actioned by mid-afternoon on the day itself; alert already self-resolved so no live impact, but the commitment is starting to slip.

Rewards GraphQL API timeout/JWT-auth errors for the wemalaysia brand. The underlying Grafana alert self-resolved overnight (19 Jul 20:39 CEST); root cause is wemalaysia's own frontend occasionally sending a malformed JWT before a valid one — known, recurring, not an FT bug. Team agreed to raise it with Ryan Yu on Monday; lead is James Hodgson.

Open breaches
  • A2/A4: agreed 'raise with Ryan Yu Monday' (18 Jul) still not actioned as of 14:39 CEST on the Monday itself — day isn't fully over but the window is closing
Proposed / pending
  • Raise JWT-generation timing with Ryan Yu Monday — agreed, not yet actioned
Timeline (6)
  • 18 Jul 16:44 UTC alert fires, Giancarlo acks within a minute
  • 18 Jul 19:08-19:13 CEST Sam Lundborg identifies root cause: wemalaysia frontend sends bad JWT before a good one
  • 18 Jul 21:21 CEST status moved to Monitoring, 'will pick this up on monday'
  • 18 Jul 22:50 CEST lead handed to James Hodgson
  • 20 Jul run: zero new Slack activity; 'raise with Ryan Yu Monday' still not actioned by early afternoon CEST — not yet a breach given the day isn't over
  • 20 Jul run (14:39 cycle): zero new Slack activity; 'raise with Ryan Yu Monday' still not actioned by mid-afternoon on the Monday itself

→ James Hodgson to message Ryan Yu today before EOD — the 'Monday' commitment is at risk of slipping.

INC-10902 slackP3open 24d· evidence to 20 Jul 11:39 UTC
H2Bet Clickhouse Writer Service Investigation

🟡 — this is very fresh (posted ~1h before this digest run) so a decision hasn't had time to land yet, but it's an important emerging pattern: a long-standing (14+ day) recurring problem now has a concrete, cheap, ready fix that's purely blocked on access, plus a fleet-wide risk across 35+ volumes that no one has acted on yet. Patrik and several senior engineers are already in the channel — worth confirming next run that the h2bet fix was applied and that someone owns reviewing the other volumes.

Recurring H2Bet nightly ClickHouse-writer pileup, previously attributed to a payload type-mismatch bug. This cycle Matteo surfaced a new, more fundamental root cause: the ClickHouse EBS volume's gp3 throughput ceiling (125MB/s) is hit during scheduled analytics scans, stalling writes fleet-wide (~40 pods crash-looping ~10x/hour for 14+ days). He has a concrete one-command fix ready but lacks the IAM permission to run it, and flagged the same risk exists on 35+ other ClickHouse volumes fleet-wide. Patrik and five senior engineers joined the channel the same moment this was posted.

Open breaches
  • A4/D12: a 14+ day recurring crash-loop is now root-caused with a ready one-line fix, but execution is blocked purely on an IAM permission gap — needs a same-day decision, not silent drift, especially given the fleet-wide exposure Matteo flagged (35+ volumes)
Proposed / pending
  • Schema/type-mismatch fix (ActionEvent.Status int vs string) — still not implemented, ownership handed to Matteo as of 17 Jul
Timeline (5)
  • 17 Jul 14:34 UTC status moved to Monitoring; Andrea's 'next week' check-in point set
  • 20 Jul 13:36 CEST Matteo posts a new, deeper root-cause finding: the h2bet ClickHouse 16TB gp3 volume is capped at default 125MB/s throughput; scheduled Hex analytics scans pin disk reads at that ceiling for 5+ min, writes collapse, and the clickhouse-writer fleet (~40 pods) times out and crash-loops together — happening ~10x/hour for 14+ days
  • 20 Jul 13:36 CEST Matteo proposes a one-command live fix (AWS EC2 modify-volume, ~$15/mo) but flags he can't run it himself — his SOC role lacks ec2:ModifyVolume
  • 20 Jul 13:36 CEST Matteo flags this is fleet-wide: 35+ ClickHouse volumes across eu1/eu2/betconstruct/wt-ltk/everymatrix clusters share the same gp3 default-throughput risk (incl. roobet 12.9TB, gamdom 4.9TB, padisahbet 4.4TB) — h2bet is just the first to become symptomatic
  • 20 Jul 13:36 CEST Patrik Potocki, Stephen Riolo, Max Holdaway, Agustín Castaño, Curt Healey and Andrew Pirotta all join the channel

→ Grant Matteo (or have someone with access run) the ec2:ModifyVolume fix for h2bet today; assign an owner to review throughput settings across the other 34 similarly-provisioned ClickHouse volumes flagged fleet-wide.

Watch-level per management note (ping-pong non-critical, B7 does not apply); no change this cycle.

frank-casino ping-pong deployment stuck in CrashLoopBackOff (403 from integration API on ping). No new activity this cycle. Per management note, the deployment ping-pong service is not critical (B7 does not apply).

Open breaches
  • D15: incident.io summary field still blank; the 403 finding remains Slack-only

INC-14331 slackP3open 4d· evidence to 18 Jul 08:36 UTC
Leonbets Activity Manager Groups Alerts Spamming

🟡 — real progress this cycle (review now actively requested from a returning senior engineer, fix validated over 4+ days), but PR #490 still isn't merged and the env vars remain a kubectl-level change exposed to reverting on the next deploy; incident.io bot's fresh check-in nudge is unanswered but too recent (~45 min) to be a breach.

Leonbets activity-manager DB-saturation alert spam; Matteo's scoped-loading fix (PR #490) proven effective over 4+ days in production on Leonbets (alerts stopped, lag under ~10k), applied via kubectl-level changes that remain exposed to reverting on the next OneClick deploy until PR #490 is merged. Matteo has now asked Malcolm Camilleri (returned from leave) to review the PR — first concrete step toward merging since it opened.

Open breaches
  • A2: durable fix merge (PR #490) still unconfirmed; kubectl-level mitigation remains revert-exposed on next OneClick deploy. Now over 5 days without a human update confirming merge status.
Proposed / pending
  • Scoped action loading (PR #490) — proven effective, merge status still unconfirmed; durable persistence of env vars still pending
Timeline (5)
  • 17 Jul 16:34 & 23:39 CEST bot check-in nudges, both unanswered
  • 18 Jul 10:37 CEST Joachim Farrugia joins the channel — no substantive update posted
  • 20 Jul run: zero new Slack activity; PR #490 merge status still unconfirmed, still over 5 days without a human update on durable merge
  • 20 Jul 13:51-13:53 CEST Matteo asks Malcolm Camilleri (back from leave) to review PR #490, confirms the image has been running clean on Leonbets since Friday — alerts stopped completely, lag stays under ~10k
  • 20 Jul 13:54 CEST incident.io bot check-in nudge asking for a status update — unanswered so far (~45 min old, not yet a breach)

→ Malcolm Camilleri to review/merge PR #490 this week so the fix becomes durable ahead of the next OneClick deploy.

INC-14567 slackP3open 2d· evidence to 20 Jul 07:41 UTC
Sub agent potential issue: dashboard-admin agent type not allowed

🟡 — lead was assigned promptly and the incident was accepted this morning, but it's now sat ~5h in Investigating with zero visible channel activity; P3, internal AI-team-only issue, no partner impact, judged leniently but now materially past the 2h+ B5 threshold.

llm-agent service rejected a dashboard-admin sub-agent invocation (allowlist restriction) plus a memory-limit query error, AI Team. Alert fired 17 Jul 22:13 CEST; Andrea assigned Jeremy Curmi as lead within ~11 minutes the same night, but the incident sat in Triage status for ~3 days until Jeremy formally accepted it into Investigating this morning (20 Jul 09:41 CEST) — this is its first appearance in the active list/digest.

Open breaches
  • B5: no status change/update for ~5h while in Investigating, no visible activity in channel
Timeline (5)
  • 17 Jul 22:13 CEST alert fires, incident channel created
  • 17 Jul 22:24-22:26 CEST Andrea Azzopardi assigns Jeremy Curmi as Incident Lead, notes merging related alarms and the need for stronger incident ownership
  • 20 Jul 09:41 CEST Jeremy Curmi formally accepts the incident, status Triage → Investigating
  • 20 Jul run: zero new Slack activity; still Investigating since 09:41 CEST accept, ~3h stale — within normal working grace period
  • 20 Jul run (14:39 cycle): zero new Slack activity; still Investigating since 09:41 CEST accept, now ~5h stale

→ Jeremy Curmi to post a status update or flag if blocked.

🟢 Under control 6

6× 🟢 routine: Betconstruct cron job nodes (P4, fix proposed 09:28 CEST, not yet applied, no partner impact), Pokerklas deposit-events gap (P1, partner still silent but FT chasing via a widening set of channels), CVE-2021-45046 base-image fix (Patrik-deferred to next week, no change), Stanleybet Romania (fix deployed, awaiting partner verification, same day), Calendars 12play door-variation (12MY confirmed by partner, TH/SG rollout in progress), 12play/we88 fire-late fix (landed, monitoring, quiet).

INC-14728 slack intercomP1open 25h· evidence to 20 Jul 12:28 UTC
[P1] Intercom Incident : Pokerklas Production

No breach — partner-side data gap continues to be chased proactively via a widening set of channels (Intercom, incident.io bot, and now a direct ask to Gustav Tjäder); P1 open ~25h with sustained FT engagement.

Pokerklas deposit-events gap (since 17 Jul) remains open; no partner reply yet on Intercom. FT continues proactively chasing via a new channel — Simon Brown asked Gustav Tjäder to nudge the partner directly, on top of the incident.io bot nudge and prior direct follow-ups. Open ~25h, not yet a breach.

Timeline (9)
  • 19 Jul 11:32 UTC P1 fires from Intercom, Joachim acks immediately
  • 19 Jul 12:40 UTC lead escalated/handed to Giancarlo Laferla
  • 19 Jul 12:59 UTC Joachim replies to partner on Intercom with confirmed root cause (missing deposit events since 17 Jul 16:51 UTC) and asks them to check internally

INC-10810 slackP3open 25d· evidence to 17 Jul 20:02 UTC
12play brands fire late

No breach — fix already landed, status honestly reflects 'Fixing' (D14), formal ownership in place, P3 judged leniently. Quiet again this cycle.

12play/we88 (Momentum) players received rewards 45-50min late due to RDS overload; a real fix landed and status has held at 'Fixing' since 10 Jul while monitoring and planning a RabbitMQ 4.x cutover. Sam Lundborg holds Incident Lead (reassigned 17 Jul). No new activity this cycle.

Proposed / pending
  • RabbitMQ 4.x cutover coordination owned by Sam Lundborg — no update this cycle
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-06-24T16:00
  • update 12play via Intercom on fix progress - discussed internally, completion not confirmed
Timeline (1)
  • 20 Jul run: zero new activity this cycle
INC-14423 slackP3open 3d· evidence to 20 Jul 06:59 UTC
Container using an image vulnerable to CVE-2021-45046

No breach — Patrik already reviewed and deferred this to next week; no new activity or regression this cycle.

Log4j CVE-2021-45046 found on a staging container. Patrik Potocki personally directed Agustín to ack and a Linear ticket to be opened so it can be tackled next week — an explicit, informed deprioritization. No new activity this cycle.

Proposed / pending
  • Upgrade log4j-core - in progress
  • Linear ticket for next-week fix - per Patrik's direction
Timeline (3)
  • Patrik Potocki is a participant (already reviewed/deferred to next week per prior green_note)
  • 20 Jul run: zero new Slack activity; Patrik's next-week deferral still holds, no regression
  • 20 Jul run (14:39 cycle): zero new Slack activity; Patrik's next-week deferral still holds
INC-13889 slack intercomP4open 6d· evidence to 17 Jul 20:01 UTC
Calendars: Orphan (but live) door variations in the database(!)

No breach — fix confirmed on the primary instance, partner kept informed at each step, remaining-instance rollout scoped with a timeframe; every partner message answered within minutes throughout.

Door-only Chinese variation bug on 12play's Promotional Calendar 3 (no player impact) — fix confirmed working on the 12MY instance by the partner (Sook); other instances (TH, SG) still show the issue, and Rewards Development is rolling the fix out to them over the coming days.

Proposed / pending
  • backoffice-v2 PR — shipped
  • rewards-backend PR — shipped
  • Roll out fix to 12play TH/SG instances — scoped, ETA 'coming days'
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-10T04:15
  • fix confirmed tested, deployed to 12play production — verified by partner 20 Jul
  • roll out to 12play TH/SG in the coming days — communicated 20 Jul
Timeline (5)
  • 17 Jul 20:01 UTC — Sam Lundborg set as Incident Lead; status moved Investigating → Fixing
  • 20 Jul 07:45 UTC Rewards Development confirms fix deployed on 12play's environment, asks partner to verify
  • 20 Jul 09:13-09:36 UTC partner (Sook) confirms the fix works on the 12MY instance
  • 20 Jul 10:11-10:33 UTC Sook flags other instances (TH, SG) still affected; Rewards Development confirms rollout to remaining instances 'in the coming days' and requests the stable platform version internally
  • 20 Jul run: zero new Slack/Intercom activity; TH/SG rollout 'in the coming days' still holds, no regression
INC-14830 slackP4open 6h· evidence to 20 Jul 07:28 UTC
Betconstruct cron job nodes

No breach — root cause found and fix proposed same morning; still not applied ~5h later but P4, internal-only, no partner impact — staleness alone isn't a breach here.

P4: LLM MCP scheduled-job cron pods failed to spawn on Betconstruct cluster nodes over the weekend. Giancarlo flagged it to Agustín/Andrew this morning; Andrew root-caused it to node DiskPressure and proposed either recycling the nodes or applying Agustín's fix (increase disk to 40GB). No partner impact — internal AI-team tooling.

Proposed / pending
  • Recycle affected nodes, or apply Agustín's fix (increase node disk to 40GB) — proposed 09:28 CEST 20 Jul, not yet applied
Timeline (4)
  • 20 Jul 06:08 UTC opened, lead self-assigned (Giancarlo) same time
  • 20 Jul 08:10 CEST Giancarlo asks Agustín/Andrew to look at weekend cron-job node failures
  • 20 Jul 09:28 CEST Andrew Pirotta root-causes it to DiskPressure on Betconstruct cluster nodes, proposes recycling nodes or applying Agustín's 40GB-disk fix
  • 20 Jul run (14:39 cycle): zero new Slack activity; Agustín's 40GB-disk fix / node recycle still not applied, ~5h10m since proposed
